from lfx.base.data.utils import IMG_FILE_TYPES, TEXT_FILE_TYPES
from lfx.base.io.chat import ChatComponent
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput
from lfx.io import (
    DropdownInput,
    FileInput,
    MessageTextInput,
    MultilineInput,
    Output,
)
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.utils.constants import (
    MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_USER,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
)


class ChatInput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Input"
    description = "Get chat inputs from the Playground."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/chat-input-and-output"
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatInput"
    minimized = True

    inputs = [
        MultilineInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Input Text",
            value="",
            info="Message to be passed as input.",
            input_types=[],
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="should_store_message",
            display_name="Store Messages",
            info="Store the message in the history.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="sender",
            display_name="Sender Type",
            options=[MESSAGE_SENDER_AI, MESSAGE_SENDER_USER],
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
            info="Type of sender.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_USER,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        FileInput(
            name="files",
            display_name="Files",
            file_types=TEXT_FILE_TYPES + IMG_FILE_TYPES,
            info="Files to be sent with the message.",
            advanced=True,
            is_list=True,
            temp_file=True,
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Chat Message", name="message", method="message_response"),
    ]

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # Ensure files is a list and filter out empty/None values
        files = self.files if self.files else []
        if files and not isinstance(files, list):
            files = [files]
        # Filter out None/empty values
        files = [f for f in files if f is not None and f != ""]

        session_id = self.session_id or self.graph.session_id or ""
        message = await Message.create(
            text=self.input_value,
            sender=self.sender,
            sender_name=self.sender_name,
            session_id=session_id,
            context_id=self.context_id,
            files=files,
        )
        if session_id and isinstance(message, Message) and self.should_store_message:
            stored_message = await self.send_message(
                message,
            )
            self.message.value = stored_message
            message = stored_message

        self.status = message
        return message

from lfx.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from lfx.custom.custom_component.component import Component
from lfx.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from lfx.io import MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.template.utils import update_template_values


class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Prompt Template"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/components-prompts"
    icon = "braces"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt Template"
    priority = 0  # Set priority to 0 to make it appear first

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        prompt = Message.from_template(**self._attributes)
        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
        frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=prompt_template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=custom_fields,
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
        )
        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        # Kept it duplicated for backwards compatibility
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
        )
        # Now that template is updated, we need to grab any values that were set in the current_frontend_node
        # and update the frontend_node with those values
        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.base.models.openai_constants import OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES, OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES[0],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        logger.debug(f"Executing request with model: {self.model_name}")
        # Handle api_key - it can be string or SecretStr
        api_key_value = None
        if self.api_key:
            logger.debug(f"API key type: {type(self.api_key)}, value: {self.api_key!r}")
            if isinstance(self.api_key, SecretStr):
                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": model_kwargs,
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }

        # TODO: Revisit if/once parameters are supported for reasoning models
        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

system_message.value:
You extract a normalized AP email packet for a finance triage workflow.

Return JSON only.

Internal reasoning rule:
- First compute a hidden symbolic trace using variables such as:
  EMAIL_LANGUAGE, VENDOR_ID_PRESENT, INVOICE_REFS, LEGAL_THREAT, PAYMENT_DEMAND, DELIVERY_HOLD_RISK, DAYS_OVERDUE_CLAIMED.
- Do not output the symbolic trace. Use it only to improve consistency of the final JSON.

JSON schema:
{
  "vendor_name": "string",
  "vendor_id": "string",
  "vendor_email": "string",
  "subject": "string",
  "invoice_ids": ["string"],
  "document_refs": ["string"],
  "days_overdue": 0,
  "language": "string",
  "request_type": "string",
  "threat_flags": ["string"],
  "urgency_signals": ["string"],
  "attachment_mentions": ["string"],
  "summary": "string"
}

Rules:
- Use only readable input text.
- Never invent vendor names, invoice IDs, dates, or amounts.
- If a field is unknown, use an empty string, empty array, or 0.
- Keep `summary` to 2 sentences max.
- `invoice_ids` must contain only IDs actually seen in the message.
- `document_refs` may include invoice IDs, credit note IDs, batch IDs, shipment refs, or vendor IDs explicitly present in the message.
- `days_overdue` must be an integer. Use 0 if unknown.
- `request_type` should be a short label such as payment_status, legal_threat, partial_payment_dispute, credit_note_issue, delivery_hold_risk, or other.

from lfx.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from lfx.custom.custom_component.component import Component
from lfx.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from lfx.io import MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.template.utils import update_template_values


class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Prompt Template"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/components-prompts"
    icon = "braces"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt Template"
    priority = 0  # Set priority to 0 to make it appear first

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        prompt = Message.from_template(**self._attributes)
        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
        frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=prompt_template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=custom_fields,
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
        )
        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        # Kept it duplicated for backwards compatibility
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
        )
        # Now that template is updated, we need to grab any values that were set in the current_frontend_node
        # and update the frontend_node with those values
        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.base.models.openai_constants import OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES, OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES[0],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        logger.debug(f"Executing request with model: {self.model_name}")
        # Handle api_key - it can be string or SecretStr
        api_key_value = None
        if self.api_key:
            logger.debug(f"API key type: {type(self.api_key)}, value: {self.api_key!r}")
            if isinstance(self.api_key, SecretStr):
                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": model_kwargs,
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }

        # TODO: Revisit if/once parameters are supported for reasoning models
        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

system_message.value:
You are Agent 1 for AP email triage.

Return JSON only.

JSON schema:
{
  "priority": "LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH|CRITICAL",
  "priority_score": 0,
  "vendor_name": "string",
  "vendor_id": "string",
  "vendor_language": "string",
  "vendor_tier": "string",
  "assigned_clerk": "string",
  "assigned_queue": "string",
  "lane": "auto|human_review_required",
  "auto_ok": true,
  "flags": ["string"],
  "document_refs": ["string"],
  "days_overdue": 0,
  "matched_case_id": "string",
  "invoice_evidence_present": true,
  "safe_to_commit": true,
  "human_review_reason": "string",
  "case_focus": "string",
  "route_confidence": 0.0,
  "routing_basis": "string",
  "why_this_clerk": "string",
  "triage_rationale": "string"
}

Rules:
- Before writing JSON, compute a hidden symbolic decision trace with variables:
  EMAIL_LANG, VENDOR_MATCH, DOC_MATCH, ATTACH_EVIDENCE, ERP_MATCH, ERP_DAYS_OVERDUE, LEGAL_THREAT, DELIVERY_HOLD_RISK, SAFE_TO_COMMIT, HITL_REQUIRED.
- Do not output that trace. Use it to enforce consistency.
- `route_confidence` MUST be a decimal from 0.0 to 1.0. NEVER use words.
- `priority_score` must be an integer 0-100.
- Use only the packet facts.
- Case isolation rule:
  only use evidence from `case_evidence_db` when the vendor identity matches and at least one document reference or vendor ID overlaps with the email.
  If that match is not present, ignore that evidence completely.
- `vendor_language` must follow the detected email language when available. Do not replace it with a preferred language from master data unless the email language is missing.
- `document_refs` must contain only invoice IDs, batch IDs, shipment refs, or equivalent identifiers explicitly present in the email.
- `days_overdue` must reflect the best supported case facts:
  1) matched ERP/case evidence when vendor and refs match
  2) explicit email fact
  3) otherwise 0.
- If `invoice_evidence_json` contains matched documents for the same vendor/refs, use those attachment facts to enrich the case focus and evidence completeness assessment.
- `matched_case_id` may only be populated when vendor identity matches and at least one document ref overlaps with the case evidence.
- `invoice_evidence_present` is true only when matched attachment evidence exists for the same vendor/refs.
- `safe_to_commit` should use matched ERP/case evidence when available; otherwise false.
- Assign the clerk using the AP clerk directory with this ranking order:
  1) language match with `vendor_language`
  2) queue fit for the case severity and lane
  3) specialty match for the complaint type
  4) escalation suitability for Tier 1 / legal / delivery-risk cases
- If legal threat or delivery hold risk is present, route to `human_review_required`.
- If Tier 1 + overdue > 30 + safe_to_commit is false, route to `human_review_required`.
- If evidence is incomplete, lower confidence and mention the blocker in `flags`.
- `human_review_reason` must be a short business explanation when lane = `human_review_required`; otherwise empty.
- `routing_basis` must explain the routing logic in plain business terms and mention the triggering business risk.
- `why_this_clerk` must explain why the selected clerk is the best operator match, explicitly referencing language and/or specialty.
- `case_focus` must be a short phrase a clerk can understand at a glance.
- Keep `triage_rationale` to 3 sentences max.
- Never produce a readable card here. Routing JSON only.

You render the final triage card for an AP operator.

Input is the triage JSON from Agent 1.

Step 1: normalize route confidence.
- If `route_confidence` is a float <= 1.0, multiply by 100 and round.
- If it is a float > 1.0, round it.
- If it is "high", use 95.
- If it is "medium", use 75.
- If it is "low", use 55.
- If missing or unknown, infer a reasonable integer from the flags and rationale.
- NEVER output `unknown%`.

Output goals:
- This is OUTPUT 0: the quick-read AP Clerk card.
- The tone must be telegraphic, operator-friendly, and immediate.
- The reader should understand in under 10 seconds: what is happening, how severe it is, and why this case landed with this clerk.
- Prefer business language over system language.
- Write for a non-technical reader.
- Avoid technical words like orchestration, packet, lane, model, pipeline, or HITL.
- Keep each line short and concrete.

Output format rules:
- Do NOT use pipe-delimited tables. Do NOT use | characters.
- Plain text only.

Required format:
[1] **Triage Card**
- What This Is: Quick AP intake card for the assigned clerk.
- Triage Verdict: value
- Priority: value (Confidence: integer%)
- Assigned To: value
- Queue: value
- Vendor: value
- Vendor Tier: value
- Working Language: value

[2] **Red Flags Triggered**
- Flag: value

Rules for flags:
- Translate internal logic into business-facing flags.
- Do not expose internal orchestration/debug labels such as case IDs, packet names, "manual notification required", or implementation jargon.
- Prefer short flags like LEGAL_THREAT, DELIVERY_HOLD_RISK, TIER_1_VENDOR, PENDING_CONFIRMATION, OVERDUE_45_DAYS.
- Do not include internal case IDs in flags.

[3] **Why It Landed Here**
- Routing Logic: value
- Clerk Match: value

Rules for prose:
- Do not address the reader as "you".
- Describe the assignment in third person, not second person.
- Keep the card free of backend phrasing such as "existing case found", "notification required", or policy-engine wording.
- Prefer plain wording such as:
  - "revisiÃ³n humana urgente" instead of "human lane"
  - "ruta automÃ¡tica" instead of "auto lane"

[4] **Immediate Clerk Read**
- Case Focus: value
- Key References: value
- Overdue Signal: value

Rules for Immediate Clerk Read:
- `Key References` should prioritize invoice IDs, vendor ID, and only the most important operational reference.
- Mention a prior case ID only if it materially helps the clerk act now; otherwise omit it.

from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.base.models.openai_constants import OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES, OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES[0],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        logger.debug(f"Executing request with model: {self.model_name}")
        # Handle api_key - it can be string or SecretStr
        api_key_value = None
        if self.api_key:
            logger.debug(f"API key type: {type(self.api_key)}, value: {self.api_key!r}")
            if isinstance(self.api_key, SecretStr):
                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": model_kwargs,
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }

        # TODO: Revisit if/once parameters are supported for reasoning models
        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

system_message.value:
You are Agent 2, the AP case packager.

Return JSON only.

JSON schema:
{
  "vendor_reply_report": "string",
  "internal_coord_report": "string",
  "case_summary_report": "string",
  "ticket_owner": "string",
  "next_steps": ["string"],
  "ticket_payload": {"key": "value"}
}

Global rules:
- Before writing the final JSON, compute a hidden symbolic state trace with variables such as:
  LANE, HITL_REQUIRED, HUMAN_DECISION_PRESENT, ATTACHMENT_EVIDENCE_PRESENT, ERP_MATCH_PRESENT, SAFE_TO_COMMIT, LEGAL_THREAT, DOCS_ALREADY_ATTACHED, INTERNAL_BLOCKER, EXTERNAL_BLOCKER.
- Do not output that trace. Use it to keep all reports mutually consistent.
- Use only the merged context.
- Determine the ACTIVE lane from `triage_json` first.
- If the active lane is `auto`, rely on `auto_lane_packet` and ignore inactive `human_review_pack` / `human_lane_packet` placeholders unless the triage explicitly says human review already happened.
- If the active lane is `human`, rely on `human_review_pack` and `human_lane_packet`; use `auto_lane_packet` only as background context.
- Never invent IDs, vendors, dates, payments, approvals, SAP findings, or reviewer actions.
- Strict case-isolation rule: never reuse invoice IDs, SAP open items, vendor IDs, or legal facts from another case.
- Only use evidence from `case_evidence_db` when the vendor identity matches and at least one document reference overlaps with `triage_json`.
- If no case-evidence match exists, explicitly stay generic instead of borrowing nearby references.
- If data is incomplete, mark the affected report as PROVISIONAL and state the missing evidence explicitly.
- Do NOT use pipe-delimited tables. Do NOT use | characters.
- Use plain text block formatting, not markdown tables.
- If attachment invoice evidence is present, do not ask the vendor to resend those invoice basics.
- Distinguish carefully:
  - missing_vendor_docs
  - missing_internal_validation
  - missing_payment_execution_decision
  Use the correct blocker in each report.

Language rules:
- `vendor_reply_report`: English only.
- `internal_coord_report`: English only.
- `case_summary_report`: English only.

Specific report requirements:
- Every report must start by explaining what the user is looking at and why it matters.
- `vendor_reply_report` is the corporate containment message. It must feel safe, professional, empathetic, and explicitly non-binding until validation is complete.
- `internal_coord_report` is the war-room brief for cross-functional coordination.
- `case_summary_report` is the executive snapshot for AP leadership / GPO viewers.
- If matched invoice evidence is present, use the attached-document facts directly and stop saying amounts/dates are unknown.
- If matched case evidence includes attachment metadata, you may mention that a simulated invoice / credit note is on file, but only for the matched case.
- If the issue is an overdue-payment legal threat and invoices are already evidenced, do not ask the vendor for extra invoice copies or generic supporting documentation unless a very specific missing item is truly required.

- `vendor_reply_report` must contain these exact sections in this order:
  [1] Vendor Email
  - What This Is: value
  - Status: PROVISIONAL or READY FOR REVIEW
  - Purpose: value
  - Why We Are Saying This: value

  [2] Draft To Send
  Subject: value
  Greeting: value
  Body:
  full vendor-facing email body in English, ready to copy/paste
  Closing: value

  [3] Conditions Before Commitment
  - Item: value

  [4] AP Notes
  - Item: value

- The `Draft To Send` must never promise payment, credit, replacement, reimbursement, acceptance of liability, or approval unless the active lane context explicitly confirms that decision.
- If the vendor asks for a credit or replacement, request only evidence relevant to that dispute type. Do not ask for remittance or payment proof unless the active lane context explicitly makes payment reconciliation part of the case.
- For overdue-payment legal threat cases, the default posture is:
  acknowledge receipt, confirm invoices located if evidenced, say internal review is urgent, avoid asking for documents already attached, and state that no payment commitment can be made until internal validation is complete.
- For overdue-payment legal threat cases, do not state as a company-validated legal fact that the invoices are definitively overdue, delinquent, or admitted debt unless the active context explicitly confirms that final internal conclusion.
- Safer phrasing examples:
  `we have located the referenced invoices`
  `the invoices are recorded in our systems and under review`
  `we cannot confirm a payment date yet`
- Avoid wording that sounds like a formal admission, such as:
  `we confirm they are overdue`
  `we acknowledge the debt`
  `we admit the delay`
- Use the assigned clerk name from `triage_json` as the default sign-off owner when appropriate.

- `internal_coord_report` must contain:
  [1] Internal Memo
  - What This Is: value
  - Current Status: value
  - Owner: value

  [2] The Issue
  - Summary: value

  [3] Immediate Action Plan
  - Item: owner/action

  [4] Escalation View
  - Item: value

  [5] Primary Blocker
  - Item: value

  [6] Why This Matters
  - Item: value

- `case_summary_report` must contain:
  [1] Case Summary
  - What This Is: value
  - Vendor: value
  - Exposure: value
  - Financials / References: value
  - SAP / System Status: value

  [2] Root Cause Hypothesis
  - Item: value

  [3] Decision Snapshot
  - Owner: value
  - Status: value
  - Next Milestone: value

Output quality rules:
- Keep `ticket_owner` short and operational.
- `next_steps` must be short action lines aligned with the ACTIVE lane only.
- `ticket_payload` must contain only high-signal operational facts actually supported by the active context.
- If ERP or attachment evidence is missing, say so explicitly instead of filling gaps with cross-case references.
- `vendor_reply_report`, `internal_coord_report`, and `case_summary_report` must agree on:
  lane, owner, evidence present, whether human review is required, and the main blocker.
- Writing style:
  - Use simple, plain language for non-technical readers.
  - Avoid developer jargon (orchestration, packet, lane, HITL, model, pipeline, debug).
  - Prefer business wording: "revisiÃ³n humana", "ruta automÃ¡tica", "bloqueo interno", "siguiente paso".

            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

system_message.value:
You infer what the human analyst likely did in the human-review lane.

Return JSON only.

JSON schema:
{
  "observed_human_decision": {"summary": "string"},
  "why_human_had_to_step_in": ["string"],
  "inferred_manual_actions_taken": ["string"],
  "implicit_assumptions": ["string"],
  "evidence_still_missing": ["string"],
  "automation_candidate_steps": ["string"],
  "governance_gaps_identified": ["string"],
  "recommended_controls": ["string"],
  "analysis_confidence": 0.0
}

Rules:
- Before writing JSON, compute a hidden symbolic trace with variables:
  HITL_REQUIRED, HUMAN_DECISION_PRESENT, HUMAN_NOTES_PRESENT, ERP_STATUS_PRESENT, ATTACHMENT_EVIDENCE_PRESENT, HUMAN_REVIEW_ALREADY_DONE.
- Do not output that trace.
- `analysis_confidence` MUST be a decimal from 0.0 to 1.0. NEVER use words.
- Use only the human-lane packet.
- First inspect `triage_json`. If the case is not actually in the human lane, return a minimal inactive result instead of inferring review work.
- Minimal inactive result means: summary = `Human review not triggered for this case.`, all lists empty, analysis_confidence = 0.0.
- If `triage_json.lane = human_review_required` but no actual human decision/notes are present, return a pending-human-review result instead of an inactive result.
- Pending-human-review result means:
  summary = `Human review required for this case, but no completed human decision is present yet.`
  `why_human_had_to_step_in` explains the trigger.
  `inferred_manual_actions_taken` stays empty.
  `implicit_assumptions` stays empty unless explicitly supported.
  `evidence_still_missing` and `automation_candidate_steps` may be populated from the packet.
  `analysis_confidence` should stay low but non-zero.
- Never import document IDs or ERP references from another case. Only use matched evidence for the same vendor / document set.
- `why_human_had_to_step_in` must explain the exception logic, not generic human effort.
- `governance_gaps_identified` must describe process/control weaknesses exposed by the case.
- `recommended_controls` must be practical operating-model improvements, not vague advice.
- Never invent systems, controls, approvals, SAP findings, or analyst decisions that are not present.
- Keep list items short and operational.

from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.base.models.openai_constants import OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES, OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES[0],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        logger.debug(f"Executing request with model: {self.model_name}")
        # Handle api_key - it can be string or SecretStr
        api_key_value = None
        if self.api_key:
            logger.debug(f"API key type: {type(self.api_key)}, value: {self.api_key!r}")
            if isinstance(self.api_key, SecretStr):
                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": model_kwargs,
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }

        # TODO: Revisit if/once parameters are supported for reasoning models
        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

system_message.value:
You write the final HITL assumptions and automation report.

Input is the structured H1 JSON.

Rules:
- English only.
- Do NOT use pipe-delimited tables. Do NOT use | characters.
- Plain text only.
- This output is for a GPO / transformation audience seeing the process for the first time.
- Explain not just what happened, but why the orchestration needed a human and what the operating model should automate next.
- If H1 indicates human review was not triggered, say that explicitly and keep sections concise without invented actions.
- If H1 indicates human review is required but still pending, say that explicitly. Do not say the case was fully automated.
- Keep the distinction explicit:
  `HITL required` is not the same as `human decision completed`.
- If human review is pending, every section must remain in pending-review mode.
- Do not describe the case as fully automated, resolved, or fully reviewed when the human decision is still outstanding.
- Write in simple language for non-technical readers.
- Avoid technical acronyms and avoid the words orchestration, HITL, pipeline, and model.

Required format:
[1] **Process Improvement & Automation Insights**
- What This Is: Analysis of why Human-In-The-Loop intervention was needed and what to improve next.

[2] **Why the Human Had to Step In**
- Item: value

[3] **What the Human Likely Did**
- Item: value

[4] **Automation Opportunities (ROI for P2P)**
- Opportunity: value
- Benefit: value

[5] **Process Governance Gaps Identified**
- Gap: value
- Recommendation: value

[6] **Missing Evidence**
- Item: value

[7] **Suggested Future-State Sequence**
- Step: value

Additional content rules:
- When review is pending, `What the Human Likely Did` must avoid implying completed actions; frame them as expected next human actions or structurally required checks.
- When review is pending, `Missing Evidence` should prioritize the missing internal decision, approvals, and root-cause clarification.

from collections.abc import Generator
from typing import Any

import orjson
from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder

from lfx.base.io.chat import ChatComponent
from lfx.helpers.data import safe_convert
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DropdownInput, HandleInput, MessageTextInput
from lfx.schema.data import Data
from lfx.schema.dataframe import DataFrame
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.schema.properties import Source
from lfx.template.field.base import Output
from lfx.utils.constants import (
    MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
)


class ChatOutput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Output"
    description = "Display a chat message in the Playground."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/chat-input-and-output"
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatOutput"
    minimized = True

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Inputs",
            info="Message to be passed as output.",
            input_types=["Data", "DataFrame", "Message"],
            required=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="should_store_message",
            display_name="Store Messages",
            info="Store the message in the history.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="sender",
            display_name="Sender Type",
            options=[MESSAGE_SENDER_AI, MESSAGE_SENDER_USER],
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
            advanced=True,
            info="Type of sender.",
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="data_template",
            display_name="Data Template",
            value="{text}",
            advanced=True,
            info="Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="clean_data",
            display_name="Basic Clean Data",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="Whether to clean data before converting to string.",
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Output Message",
            name="message",
            method="message_response",
        ),
    ]

    def _build_source(self, id_: str | None, display_name: str | None, source: str | None) -> Source:
        source_dict = {}
        if id_:
            source_dict["id"] = id_
        if display_name:
            source_dict["display_name"] = display_name
        if source:
            # Handle case where source is a ChatOpenAI object
            if hasattr(source, "model_name"):
                source_dict["source"] = source.model_name
            elif hasattr(source, "model"):
                source_dict["source"] = str(source.model)
            else:
                source_dict["source"] = str(source)
        return Source(**source_dict)

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # First convert the input to string if needed
        text = self.convert_to_string()

        # Get source properties
        source, _, display_name, source_id = self.get_properties_from_source_component()

        # Create or use existing Message object
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Message) and not self.is_connected_to_chat_input():
            message = self.input_value
            # Update message properties
            message.text = text
            # Preserve existing session_id from the incoming message if it exists
            existing_session_id = message.session_id
        else:
            message = Message(text=text)
            existing_session_id = None

        # Set message properties
        message.sender = self.sender
        message.sender_name = self.sender_name
        # Preserve session_id from incoming message, or use component/graph session_id
        message.session_id = (
            self.session_id or existing_session_id or (self.graph.session_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None) or ""
        )
        message.context_id = self.context_id
        message.flow_id = self.graph.flow_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None
        message.properties.source = self._build_source(source_id, display_name, source)

        # Store message if needed
        if message.session_id and self.should_store_message:
            stored_message = await self.send_message(message)
            self.message.value = stored_message
            message = stored_message

        self.status = message
        return message

    def _serialize_data(self, data: Data) -> str:
        """Serialize Data object to JSON string."""
        # Convert data.data to JSON-serializable format
        serializable_data = jsonable_encoder(data.data)
        # Serialize with orjson, enabling pretty printing with indentation
        json_bytes = orjson.dumps(serializable_data, option=orjson.OPT_INDENT_2)
        # Convert bytes to string and wrap in Markdown code blocks
        return "```json\n" + json_bytes.decode("utf-8") + "\n```"

    def _validate_input(self) -> None:
        """Validate the input data and raise ValueError if invalid."""
        if self.input_value is None:
            msg = "Input data cannot be None"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list) and not all(
            isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str) for item in self.input_value
        ):
            invalid_types = [
                type(item).__name__
                for item in self.input_value
                if not isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str)
            ]
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, got {invalid_types}"
            raise TypeError(msg)
        if not isinstance(
            self.input_value,
            Message | Data | DataFrame | str | list | Generator | type(None),
        ):
            type_name = type(self.input_value).__name__
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, Generator or None, got {type_name}"
            raise TypeError(msg)

    def convert_to_string(self) -> str | Generator[Any, None, None]:
        """Convert input data to string with proper error handling."""
        self._validate_input()
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list):
            clean_data: bool = getattr(self, "clean_data", False)
            return "\n".join([safe_convert(item, clean_data=clean_data) for item in self.input_value])
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Generator):
            return self.input_value
        return safe_convert(self.input_value)

    MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
)


class ChatOutput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Output"
    description = "Display a chat message in the Playground."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/chat-input-and-output"
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatOutput"
    minimized = True

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Inputs",
            info="Message to be passed as output.",
            input_types=["Data", "DataFrame", "Message"],
            required=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="should_store_message",
            display_name="Store Messages",
            info="Store the message in the history.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="sender",
            display_name="Sender Type",
            options=[MESSAGE_SENDER_AI, MESSAGE_SENDER_USER],
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
            advanced=True,
            info="Type of sender.",
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="data_template",
            display_name="Data Template",
            value="{text}",
            advanced=True,
            info="Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="clean_data",
            display_name="Basic Clean Data",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="Whether to clean data before converting to string.",
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Output Message",
            name="message",
            method="message_response",
        ),
    ]

    def _build_source(self, id_: str | None, display_name: str | None, source: str | None) -> Source:
        source_dict = {}
        if id_:
            source_dict["id"] = id_
        if display_name:
            source_dict["display_name"] = display_name
        if source:
            # Handle case where source is a ChatOpenAI object
            if hasattr(source, "model_name"):
                source_dict["source"] = source.model_name
            elif hasattr(source, "model"):
                source_dict["source"] = str(source.model)
            else:
                source_dict["source"] = str(source)
        return Source(**source_dict)

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # First convert the input to string if needed
        text = self.convert_to_string()

        # Get source properties
        source, _, display_name, source_id = self.get_properties_from_source_component()

        # Create or use existing Message object
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Message) and not self.is_connected_to_chat_input():
            message = self.input_value
            # Update message properties
            message.text = text
            # Preserve existing session_id from the incoming message if it exists
            existing_session_id = message.session_id
        else:
            message = Message(text=text)
            existing_session_id = None

        # Set message properties
        message.sender = self.sender
        message.sender_name = self.sender_name
        # Preserve session_id from incoming message, or use component/graph session_id
        message.session_id = (
            self.session_id or existing_session_id or (self.graph.session_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None) or ""
        )
        message.context_id = self.context_id
        message.flow_id = self.graph.flow_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None
        message.properties.source = self._build_source(source_id, display_name, source)

        # Store message if needed
        if message.session_id and self.should_store_message:
            stored_message = await self.send_message(message)
            self.message.value = stored_message
            message = stored_message

        self.status = message
        return message

    def _serialize_data(self, data: Data) -> str:
        """Serialize Data object to JSON string."""
        # Convert data.data to JSON-serializable format
        serializable_data = jsonable_encoder(data.data)
        # Serialize with orjson, enabling pretty printing with indentation
        json_bytes = orjson.dumps(serializable_data, option=orjson.OPT_INDENT_2)
        # Convert bytes to string and wrap in Markdown code blocks
        return "```json\n" + json_bytes.decode("utf-8") + "\n```"

    def _validate_input(self) -> None:
        """Validate the input data and raise ValueError if invalid."""
        if self.input_value is None:
            msg = "Input data cannot be None"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list) and not all(
            isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str) for item in self.input_value
        ):
            invalid_types = [
                type(item).__name__
                for item in self.input_value
                if not isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str)
            ]
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, got {invalid_types}"
            raise TypeError(msg)
        if not isinstance(
            self.input_value,
            Message | Data | DataFrame | str | list | Generator | type(None),
        ):
            type_name = type(self.input_value).__name__
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, Generator or None, got {type_name}"
            raise TypeError(msg)

    def convert_to_string(self) -> str | Generator[Any, None, None]:
        """Convert input data to string with proper error handling."""
        self._validate_input()
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list):
            clean_data: bool = getattr(self, "clean_data", False)
            return "\n".join([safe_convert(item, clean_data=clean_data) for item in self.input_value])
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Generator):
            return self.input_value
        return safe_convert(self.input_value)

START
These blocks are reference data used by the flow:
vendors, AP assignees, contacts, rules, and case evidence.

STEP 1
Read the incoming email and convert it to a clean text packet.
At the same time, extract invoice evidence from message + files.

STEP 2
Build triage JSON with priority, owner, and route.
Then render a quick clerk card for immediate action.

STEP 3
Route decision:
- Auto path for safe/low-risk cases
- Human review path for legal or high-risk cases

STEP 4
Create the final case pack and prepare three clear outputs:
Vendor email, internal memo, and leadership summary.

DELIVERABLES
OUTPUT 0-4 are the business-ready results.
DEBUG 5-6 are optional checks for quality review.

HUMAN REVIEW EXPLAINER
This branch explains why human review is needed,
what evidence is still missing, and what to automate next.
